description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a broad, wedge-shaped steel blade. The handle appears to be made from a single piece of wood, showing some wear and discoloration, likely from frequent use. The blade is wide and flat, with a straight cutting edge and a thick poll, secured to the handle with visible metal fasteners. The metal head has a weathered, matte finish, indicating exposure to the elements or heavy use. The overall design is utilitarian, with no decorative elements, making it suitable for chopping wood or other heavy-duty tasks. This axe would commonly be found in urban garages, maintenance sheds, or used by municipal workers for landscaping and emergency purposes.
description: A weathered hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, flat steel blade, showing signs of frequent use. The utilitarian design is practical for chopping tasks in urban environments.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the curved wooden handle and the broad, wedge-shaped steel blade.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the narrow edge of the blade and the rounded end of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other face of the blade and handle, with similar wear patterns.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the thickness of the blade and the alignment of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with an oval cross-section, smooth grip, and no additional grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, wedge-shaped steel blade with a straight edge and thick poll, no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible metal fasteners securing the blade to the handle.
